Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss/Alt. Stay, The Action On Grounds Of Forum Non Conveniens
Off Calendar May-02-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
James Taylor-Copeland, Esq., Taylor-Copeland, Telephone 619-734-8770, appearing for Plaintiff;
Max B. Twine, Esq., Taylor & Patchen, Telephone 415-788-8200, appearing for Defendant Enigma MPC, Inc.;

RULING - A R G U E D. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT ENIGMA MPC, INC.'s Motion To Dismiss/Alternatively to Stay, The Action On Grounds Of Forum Non Conveniens.

Defendant Enigma MPC, Inc.'s motion to dismiss this action based on forum non conveniens is granted. Plaintiff Oz Nathan and Defendant Guy Zyskind are both Israeli residents. Although Enigma is a Delaware corporation with its principal place of business in California, both it and Defendant Can Kisagun have consented to jurisdiction in Israel for the purpose of resolving this dispute and to the tolling of any applicable statutes of limitation during the pendency of the action in California. Enigma has also agreed to produce any of its three California employees in Israel. Israel therefore is a suitable alternative forum. (See Stangvik v. Shiley Inc. (1991) 54 Cal.3d 744, 752 ["Defendants stipulated that they would submit to jurisdiction in Sweden or Norway, respectively, as well as to the tolling of the statute of limitations during the pendency of the actions in California. Thus, the courts of Sweden and Norway present suitable forums for trial of the actions."].) Further, the balance of public and private factors favors Israel. The key witnesses in this case (Plaintiff and Defendant Guy Zyskind) both reside in Israel, it appears that the case arises out of actions that took place in large part in Israel, that most potential third-party witnesses reside in Israel, and that the majority of key documents are likely to be located in Israel. Finally, any judgment in this action could be most efficiently enforced in Israel, where Mr. Zyskind lives and the majority of his personal assets are located. California has a limited interest in resolving a dispute between two Israeli citizens concerning the management of a Delaware corporation nominally resident in California but amenable to suit in Israel.

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Compelling Further Responses To Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Documents, Special Interrogatories, And Form Interrogatories, And Imposing Monetary Sanctions Against Enigma
Off Calendar May-02-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
James Taylor-Copeland, esq. (619-734-8770) for Plaintiff.
Max B. Twine, esq. (415-788-8200) of Taylor & Patchen LLP for Defendant.

Not argued.

PLAINTIFF OZ NATHAN's Motion For An Order Compelling Further Responses To Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Documents, Special Interrogatories, And Form Interrogatories, And Imposing Monetary Sanctions Against Enigma is off calendar per the Court granting defendant's motion to dismiss/alt. stay action.

Judge Pro Tem: William Lynn, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
